rulings.Case Title: JOHN COYLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant Hajoca Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence that Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. Mr. Ay does not provide any foundation for his apparent opinion that from 1959-1964 or 1965 gaskets that Mr. Coyle was exposed to that were supplied by defendant that were identified with no greater specificity than the words ¿Cranite¿ or ¿Garlock¿ contained asbestos. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN COYLE VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant SVI Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satsified its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence that Mr. Coyle was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (valve bonnet gaskets) attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration and exhibit F to Mr. Archer¿s declaration (Stockham¿s specification drawings) are sustained. The court did not rule on defendant¿s objections to Mr. Emery¿s declaration. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. LEWIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. Because defendant did not explore the answer given by plaintiff in his deposition at lines 15 and 16 of page 663, the court cannot say that plaintiff has no evidence regarding asbestos content of the fireproofing that he says he was exposed to at Stanford. Even if defendant had satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. The objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RILEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant B.T. Mancini Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to asbestos attributable to defendant. The court did not consider the evidence submitted with the reply papers. Defendant¿s GO 129 responses regarding asbestos content of partitions neither prevents defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar nor creates a triable issue. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Bauer¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Brumley¿s depositon testimony, Mr. Casey¿s deposition testimony and declaration, and Mr. Kirk¿s deposition testimony create triable issues whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing Kaiser Gypsum drywall mud. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Bauer and plaintiff and the deposition testimony of Mr. Brumley are overruled. The court did not consider the lengthy declaration of Mr. Cohen. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FLOYD CZARNICK et al VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, November 2, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 12. Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence that the insulation that Mr. Czarnick removed from the Foster Wheeler boilers on the USS Ramsey is attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to paragraph 26 of Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
